vulnerability.title: Add noexec Option to /home
vulnerability.description: The noexec mount option can be used to prevent binaries from being executed out of /home. Add the noexec option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /home.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /home has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/home")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/home' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only requires editing /etc/fstab to add the noexec option for the /home mount point and optionally remounting the filesystem. This is a standard STIG hardening step with minimal risk of locking out users or breaking critical services.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential impact on user scripts or binaries stored in /home; Possible need to restart user sessions

review_feedback: The proposed sed command is generic and will edit the mount options for every entry in /etc/fstab, not just the /home line. This can add the noexec option to critical filesystems (e.g., /, /var, /usr) where execution of binaries is required, leading to service failures or boot problems. It also does not check for existing noexec entries, causing duplicates, and no backup of fstab is made, risking loss of a correct configuration.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly identifies the need to add the noexec option to the /home mount point and includes a remount step to apply the change without a reboot. However, the provided sed command is generic and would modify the mount options for all entries in /etc/fstab, potentially causing unintended side effects and duplicate noexec entries. A more targeted approach that only edits the /home line and checks for existing noexec would be safer.
concerns: The sed command as written applies to every line in /etc/fstab, not just the /home entry.
It does not check whether the noexec option is already present, leading to possible duplicate entries.
No backup of /etc/fstab is made before editing, which could risk data loss if the command fails.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of /etc/fstab before making any changes (e.g., cp /etc/fstab /etc/fstab.bak).
Use a more precise command to target only the /home line, such as: sed -i -E '/^\/home\s+/ s|\([^\s]+\)|\1,noexec|g' /etc/fstab
Add a check to avoid duplicate noexec, e.g., using grep -q 'noexec' on the options field before appending.
Validate that the /home entry exists and that the mount options field is correctly formatted before applying the edit.
